pi_items.source: Task description (line 13-14 of setup_task.sh), task.json metadata field. Cross-referenced with learneconometrics.com POE5 documentation and gretl data repository. Similar reference found in import_time_series_csv task which attempts to load from /opt/gretl_data/poe5/usa.gdt.

Confirmed in task.json description (line 5), setup_task.sh line 15, and task description explicitly states 'run an ADF test on the gdp variable'. GDP is a standard macroeconomic variable in FRED and POE5 datasets.

Verified through gretl command reference at gretl.sourceforge.net/gretl-help/cmdref.html. The 'adf' command in Gretl computes Augmented Dickey-Fuller tests. Task setup mentions 'Variable > Unit root tests > Augmented Dickey-Fuller test' (line 19 setup_task.sh).

Confirmed in task.json metadata field line 20, task description line 5, and setup_task.sh line 24. Path syntax is valid for Linux. Directory structure /home/ga/Documents/gretl_output is created by task_utils.sh (line 134).
setup_task.sh line 14 explicitly states 'Dataset: US quarterly macroeconomic data 1984Q1-2009Q3'

pi_items.metadata_value: real GDP in billions of chained 2005 dollars

setup_task.sh line 15 states 'inf (CPI inflation rate)'. Task description line 5 states 'inf (CPI inflation rate, annualized %)'

pi_items.metadata_value: Federal Reserve Bank of St. Louis FRED database, via Hill et al. POE5
pi_items.verified_value: Federal Reserve Bank of St. Louis FRED database via Principles of Econometrics (5th Edition by Hill, Griffiths, Lim)
pi_items.source: Verified through web search: (1) FRED is maintained by Federal Reserve Bank of St. Louis (fred.stlouisfed.org); (2) POE5 (Principles of Econometrics, 5th Edition) is by R. Carter Hill, William E. Griffiths, and Guay C. Lim; (3) Lee Adkins provides POE5 data for Gretl at learneconometrics.com; (4) POE5 documentation confirms data files are sourced from FRED and other government sources; (5) Gretl distribution documentation states it includes 'US macroeconomic time series from the Federal Reserve Bank of St Louis'

privileged_info_summary: The task runs an Augmented Dickey-Fuller (ADF) unit root test on the 'gdp' variable from the usa.gdt dataset. The dataset contains US quarterly macroeconomic data spanning 1984Q1 to 2009Q3, with two key variables: 'gdp' (real GDP in billions of chained 2005 dollars) and 'inf' (CPI inflation rate, annualized %). The data source is confirmed as the Federal Reserve Bank of St. Louis FRED database accessed through Principles of Econometrics, 5th Edition (Hill, Griffiths, Lim). The expected output file is /home/ga/Documents/gretl_output/adf_test_results.txt. The Augmented Dickey-Fuller test type is correctly identified. All dataset characteristics, variable names, time periods, and data sources are verified or confirmed through task implementation scripts and authoritative econometrics sources.
